items.headline: Flood Watch issued April 22 at 12:36PM AKDT until April 25 at 11:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by rain and snowmelt is possible.

* WHERE...The following areas, Central Susitna Valley, Southern
Susitna Valley and Western Susitna Valley.  This includes the
communities of Skwentna, Willow, Talkeetna, Trapper Creek, And
Petersville.

* WHEN...From 9 PM AKDT this evening through Saturday morning.

* IMPACTS...Flooding of creeks, streams, low-lying and flood-prone
locations will be possible as a prolonged period of rainfall is
expected Wednesday night into Saturday mor
items.areas: Western Susitna Valley; Southern Susitna Valley; Central Susitna Valley
items.instruction:
Persons living along streams should stay alert and be prepared to
take action if a warning or advisory is issued, or flooding is
observed.

You should monitor later forecasts and be alert for possible Flood
Warnings. Those living in areas prone to flooding should be prepared
to take action should fl

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 22 at 12:24PM AKDT until April 23 at 1:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow expected. Total snow accumulations up
to two inches. Winds gusting as high as 40 mph.

* WHERE...Dalton Highway Summits.

* WHEN...From 4 PM this afternoon to 1 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted. Areas of blowing snow could
significantly reduce visibility.
items.instruction:
Slow down and use caution while traveling. Visit 511.alaska.gov or
call 5 1 1 for the latest road conditions.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 22 at 12:22PM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow expected. Total snow accumulations
between 1 and 2 inches. Winds gusting as high as 35 mph.

* WHERE...Central Brooks Range.

* WHEN...From 7 PM this evening to 4 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted. Areas of blowing snow could
significantly reduce visibility.
items.instruction:
Slow down and use caution while traveling. Visit 511.alaska.gov or
call 5 1 1 for the latest road conditions.

items.headline: Blizzard Warning issued April 22 at 12:16PM AKDT until April 23 at 10:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Blizzard conditions. Additional snow accumulations between
1 and 3 inches. Winds gusting as high as 40 mph.

* WHERE...Baldwin Peninsula, Lower Kobuk Valley, and Northern Seward
Peninsula.

* WHEN...Until 10 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest winds will arrive midday/early
afternoon today, continuing through tonight. Wind gusts will
remain around 35 mph through Thursday night.
items.areas: Lower Kobuk Valley; Baldwin Peninsula; Northern Seward Peninsula
items.instruction:
Travel should be restricted to emergencies only. If you must travel,
have a winter survival kit with you. If you get stranded, stay with
your vehicle.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 22 at 12:16PM AKDT until April 23 at 10:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:

Winds gusting as high as 40 mph.

items.areas: Yukon Delta Coast; Lower Yukon River

items.headline: Blizzard Warning issued April 22 at 12:16PM AKDT until April 23 at 10:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Winds are expected to weaken later this

items.headline: Blizzard Warning issued April 22 at 12:16PM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Blizzard conditions. Additional snow accumulations between
4 and 6 inches. Winds gusting as high as 55 mph.

* WHERE...St Lawrence Island.

* WHEN...Until 4 AM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult. Areas of blowing snow
could significantly reduce visibility. Strong winds could result
in power outages.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest wind will continue through the
afternoon. Afterwards, wind gusts will remain elevated, up to 45
mph through Thursday afternoon. Temper
items.instruction:
Travel should be restricted to emergencies only. If you must travel,
have a winter survival kit with you. If you get stranded, stay with
your vehicle.

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 22 at 11:48AM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 35 to 45 mph with gusts up to 65 mph expected.

* WHERE...Northern Denali Borough.

* WHEN...From 10 PM this evening to 4 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.
